title: Fragrant Nutty Ukrainian Buckwheat Pilaf
description: A hearty and aromatic Ukrainian dish featuring buckwheat, cardamom, and kombu cold extract, perfect for a weeknight dinner. This pilaf is nutty, chewy, and packed with anti-inflammatory friendly ingredients.
instructions: Rinse the buckwheat groats and drain well.
In a large pot, bring the water to a boil and add the buckwheat groats, reducing heat to low and simmering for 15 minutes.
In a small bowl, mix the kombu cold extract, crushed cardamom pods, salt, and black pepper.
In a large skillet, saute the chopped onion, grated carrots, and sliced mushrooms over medium heat until the vegetables are tender.
Add the cooked buckwheat groats to the skillet and stir to combine with the vegetables and spice mixture.
Lay a ti leaf flat and place 1/4 of the buckwheat mixture in the center of the leaf.
Fold the ti leaf over the filling to form a square package and repeat with the remaining ingredients.
Steam the ti leaf packages over boiling water for 10 minutes, serving hot.
